name: MATH_WORKFLOW
description: Mathematical QA workflow for GNOSTICA validation. One unit of work = one Putnam problem. Workers attempt to solve problems using GNOSTICA, identify capability gaps, and produce structured QA findings. Output feeds RDC_WORKFLOW for consolidation, then SDLC_WORKFLOW for implementation. Triggered by phrase 'MATH_WORKFLOW'. Owner: Michael.

on_engage: QUEEN reads this JSON in full
QUEEN reports: 'MATH_WORKFLOW mode engaged. Ready. Specify problem range or assignment strategy.'
QUEEN waits for explicit direction: problem IDs, year range, topic filter, or 'all'

relationship_to_other_workflows.contract: MATH_WORKFLOW produces per-problem QA reports documenting GNOSTICA gaps. These reports become the 'source documents' for RDC_WORKFLOW, which consolidates them into PROJECT/PHASE docs. SDLC_WORKFLOW then executes fixes.
relationship_to_other_workflows.handoff: On MATH batch completion, QUEEN triggers RDC_WORKFLOW with output directory as source.

outputs_per_unit: append to docs/qa/GAPS_REGISTRY.md — any new gaps discovered

QUEEN.description: Claude in main conversation. Manages problem assignment, spawns MATHQA workers, tracks progress, aggregates results.
responsibilities: INVENTORY: query database for problem set, determine assignment strategy
ASSIGNMENT: partition problems across workers (parallel batches or sequential)
SPAWN: launch MATHQA workers with problem assignments
TRACK: maintain MATH_PROGRESS.md with per-problem status
AGGREGATE: after batch completion, produce GAPS_SUMMARY.md
HANDOFF: trigger RDC_WORKFLOW with qa/attempts/ as source directory

MATHQA.description: Core worker. Given one problem ID, retrieves problem + solution, extracts mathematical operations, attempts each in GNOSTICA, documents results.
discipline: Read solution from Kedlaya TeX (search for \item[{SESSION}{NUMBER}])
Extract discrete mathematical operations from solution
Attempt each operation in GNOSTICA via REPL or eval
Identify root cause for failures (missing rule, parser issue, etc.)
Produce actionable gap description suitable for DEV consumption

gap_taxonomy.description: Classification of GNOSTICA gaps discovered during MATHQA passes

PARSER.description: Input parsing failures — GNOSTICA can't understand the expression

RULE_MISSING.description: No rule exists for the required operation

RULE_MISMATCH.description: Rule exists but pattern doesn't match parser output AST

INCOMPLETE_SIMPLIFICATION.description: Operation runs but result isn't fully simplified

WRONG_RESULT.description: Operation runs but produces incorrect output

PERFORMANCE.description: Operation is too slow for practical use

UX.description: Operation works but interface is confusing or output is unreadable

gaps_registry.description: Master list of all discovered GNOSTICA gaps across all problems
gaps_registry.format: One entry per unique gap, with: ID, category, description, affected problems, severity, suggested fix

gaps_summary.description: Statistical summary produced by QUEEN after batch completion
gaps_summary.format: Counts by category, severity distribution, most common gaps, priority ranking

math_progress.description: Running progress tracker during MATH_WORKFLOW execution
math_progress.format: Per-problem status: NOT_STARTED | IN_PROGRESS | COMPLETE | BLOCKED

actions: Query database for problem set based on user criteria (year, topic, all)
Determine parallelism strategy (batch size, worker count)
Initialize MATH_PROGRESS.md with all problems as NOT_STARTED
Report to human: N problems queued, proposed batch strategy

SEQUENTIAL.description: One problem at a time. Use for initial testing or when context matters.

PARALLEL_BATCH.description: Multiple workers processing independent problems simultaneously.
PARALLEL_BATCH.parallelism: all workers in one message with run_in_background:true

TOPIC_PARTITIONED.description: One worker per topic. Useful for focused gap discovery.
TOPIC_PARTITIONED.parallelism: all topic workers in one message

per_problem: QUEEN spawns MATHQA with problem ID
MATHQA appends new gaps to GAPS_REGISTRY.md
MATHQA returns structured result to QUEEN

actions: Read all QA reports from docs/qa/attempts/
Count problems by pass rate, gaps by category
Produce priority-ranked gap list for RDC input
Report to human: N problems tested, M unique gaps found, top 10 gaps listed
Recommend: 'Ready for RDC_WORKFLOW with docs/qa/attempts/ as source'

main_sequence: INVENTORY: QUEEN queries database, initializes tracking docs
EXECUTION: MATHQA workers process problems, produce QA reports
AGGREGATION: QUEEN consolidates gaps, produces summary
HANDOFF: QUEEN triggers RDC_WORKFLOW for gap consolidation

rdc_integration.scribe_behavior: Each QA report is one source doc. SCRIBE extracts gaps as concepts, upserts into MASTER.

phases: PHASE_1_CRITICAL_BLOCKERS — gaps that prevent any problem solving
PHASE_3_RULE_EXPANSION — new rules for missing operations
taxonomy_output.todo_format: Each gap becomes a task with: description, affected problems, acceptance criteria (problem N passes)

sdlc_integration.task_acceptance: Task is done when the specific Putnam problem(s) that revealed the gap now PASS
sdlc_integration.validation_loop: After SDLC task completion, re-run MATHQA on affected problems to verify fix

hard_rules.no_manual_math_solving: Workers use GNOSTICA, not their own math abilities
hard_rules.gaps_must_be_actionable: Every gap must include enough detail for a DEV to implement a fix
hard_rules.deduplicate_gaps: Same gap from multiple problems = one registry entry with multiple affected-problems
hard_rules.preserve_problem_traceability: Every gap links back to the problem(s) that revealed it

notes.purpose: MATH_WORKFLOW is a gap-discovery engine. It uses the Putnam corpus as a test suite to systematically find what GNOSTICA cannot do. The gaps become work items for GNOSTICA development.
notes.not_about_solving_math: Workers are NOT solving Putnam problems. They are testing whether GNOSTICA can perform the operations that the published solutions require.
notes.incremental: Can run on subsets (one year, one topic) to focus gap discovery. Full corpus run is optional.
notes.rdc_ratchet: Each MATH_WORKFLOW run produces more source docs for RDC. RDC consolidates all gaps discovered so far. SDLC fixes them. Next MATH run finds fewer gaps. Ratchet toward full coverage.
